content.assessment: Viewed through the Polish reform lens, this proposal preserves the existing structural mechanism (the 6/8-year track boundary) and instead attempts to alter selection procedures within it. Poland's experience indicates that the structural mechanism itself — the age at which tracking occurs — was the primary driver of measured equity gains in 1999–2015, while procedural tinkering was not the tested variable. A reform that leaves the tracking point untouched forgoes the specific lever Polish evidence associates with distributional improvement, and instead targets a distinct mechanism (admissions procedure bias) that Polish data cannot speak to directly. Because there is no structural change, reversibility risk in the Polish sense (abrupt reversal of a structural boundary) is largely absent, but the proposal also does not engage the primary-school-choice segregation channel that Polish-adjacent evidence identifies as an independent mechanism.
opportunities: Could serve as a lower-risk complement to future structural change, addressing distributional effects at the margin without the implementation disruption Poland experienced (F-live-polish_reform-05)
strengths: Avoids the single-year implementation shock risk documented in Poland's 2017 reversal, since no structural boundary is being moved (F-live-polish_reform-05)
Low reversibility risk relative to structural approaches, since procedural rules are easier to adjust incrementally than track boundaries (F-live-polish_reform-04)
threats: Because it does not touch the structural mechanism, any equity gains may be more easily eroded or reversed administratively without the political salience of a structural law change, unlike Poland's visible 1999/2017 legislative acts (F-live-polish_reform-04)
weaknesses: Does not alter the age-10/12 tracking point that Polish evidence associates with the structural mechanism producing measured equity gains, so the reform lever tested in Poland is absent here (F-live-polish_reform-03)

Leaves the primary-school-choice segregation mechanism wholly unaddressed, a gap explicitly noted as a limitation and one that structural reform elsewhere has had to bundle with anti-sorting rules (F-live-polish_reform-07)
